skill.name: amap-lbs-skill
skill.description: Use AMap Web Service APIs for POI search, route planning, travel planning, and map visualization.
skill.summary: Find places, plan routes, and create AMap visualization links from user location requests.
skill.author: AMap-Web

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, environment, and obfuscation patterns. Review confirmed legitimate network use and local key handling, while shell execution, weak cryptography, obfuscation, path traversal, and prompt injection were not supported by the inspected code. The main residual risks are disclosure of AMap keys and user location data through API calls, config storage, logs, and generated URLs.

medium_findings.title: Plaintext API Key Storage
medium_findings.description: The skill reads and writes the AMap Web Service key in config.json. This is legitimate configuration handling, but a plaintext local key can be exposed through file access, backups, or accidental sharing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly persists config.webServiceKey to a local JSON file. This is an intentional feature, but it creates a clear secret-handling risk.

medium_findings.title: Location And Key Data Sent To AMap APIs
medium_findings.description: The skill sends API keys, search terms, locations, origins, destinations, and city names to AMap REST endpoints. This behavior is expected for a mapping skill, but users should treat it as third-party data disclosure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are explicit and route or POI data is included in request parameters. The destination is the expected AMap service, so the risk is privacy disclosure rather than malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Location Data Embedded In Generated URLs
medium_findings.description: Generated visualization and search links include encoded route, POI, coordinate, or heatmap data in URL query strings. These URLs can leak sensitive locations through browser history, referrers, logs, or sharing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The link generation code and instructions intentionally place map task data or coordinates in query parameters. This is functional but exposes data if links are shared or logged.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static command-execution alerts correspond to template literals, documentation code fences, CLI examples, and console output. No child_process, exec, spawn, eval, or shell invocation was found in the inspected implementation files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are string interpolation or documentation examples rather than shell execution APIs. I found no evidence of dynamic command execution.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts came from require("../index") imports in scripts and not from user-controlled file paths. The config path is fixed with __dirname and config.json.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative imports are static module references. There is no user-controlled file path or directory traversal read/write primitive in the reviewed code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Obfuscation Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and entropy findings appear to be false positives from ordinary package metadata, Chinese documentation, coordinate examples, and strings. No cryptographic implementation, encoded payload, or obfuscated loader was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain JavaScript and Markdown content, not encrypted blobs or cryptographic code. The entropy signal is plausible for multilingual text and dense examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Network Requests With User Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill makes HTTPS requests to AMap services with API keys and user-supplied mapping parameters. This is necessary for the advertised function but requires clear user awareness.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and central to the skill. It is a confirmed data disclosure pattern to the intended third-party service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Plaintext Secret Persistence
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can store the AMap key in config.json without encryption or permission hardening.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path serializes the complete config object, including webServiceKey. This is confirmed by the save and set key functions.

content.value_statement: Location work often requires separate searches, route calls, and map links. This skill combines AMap POI search, route planning, travel planning, and visualization workflows.
actual_capabilities: Searches AMap POI data by keyword, city, type, location, radius, page, and result count.
Plans walking, driving, bicycling, and public transit routes between coordinate pairs.
Builds travel plans by searching interest categories and connecting locations in sequence.
Generates AMap web visualization links for route and POI task data.
Creates heatmap viewer links from user-provided JSON data URLs.
Reads an AMap key from environment variables or a local config file.
limitations: Requires a valid AMap Web Service key for API-backed searches and route planning.
Works best with Chinese AMap coverage, coordinate formats, and supported AMap route modes.
Generated visualization links may expose encoded location data to AMap when opened.
The travel planner is simple and does not optimize routes by time, distance, or opening hours.

use_cases.title: Find Nearby Services
use_cases.description: Search for restaurants, hotels, stations, or other POIs near a city or coordinate and review key details.

use_cases.title: Compare Route Options
use_cases.description: Generate walking, driving, cycling, or transit plans between known coordinate pairs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Map Visualizations
use_cases.description: Create AMap links that show POI points, planned routes, or heatmap data on a web map.

prompt_templates.title: Search A City
prompt_templates.prompt: Search AMap for coffee shops in Hangzhou and show the names, addresses, and coordinates.

prompt_templates.title: Find Nearby Places
prompt_templates.prompt: Find hotels within 1000 meters of 116.397428,39.90923 and summarize the closest useful results.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a walking route from 116.397428,39.90923 to 116.427281,39.903719 and include distance and estimated time.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Travel Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-day Beijing travel plan for landmarks, food, and hotels, then generate an AMap visualization link.

output_examples.input: Find restaurants near Beijing South Railway Station.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a short list of matching restaurants with names, addresses, categories, phone data when available, and coordinates.

output_examples.input: Plan a driving route between two coordinate pairs.
output_examples.output: The skill reports distance, estimated time, toll information when available, traffic light count when available, and an AMap route link.

output_examples.input: Make a heatmap from this public JSON data URL.
output_examples.output: The skill creates an AMap heatmap viewer link using the selected map style and the encoded data URL.

best_practices: Use environment variables for the AMap key instead of saving secrets in config.json.
Provide precise city names or longitude,latitude coordinates for route and nearby searches.
Review generated map links before sharing them because they can include location or route data.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private AMap keys into prompts or shared chat logs.
Do not use this skill for emergency routing or safety-critical navigation decisions.
Do not publish generated visualization URLs when the encoded data contains sensitive locations.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. API-backed POI search and route planning require an AMap Web Service key.

faq.question: Where does it read the key from?
faq.answer: It checks AMAP_KEY, AMAP_WEBSERVICE_KEY, and a local config.json file.

faq.question: Can it plan different route types?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports walking, driving, bicycling, and public transit route calls.

faq.question: Can it create map links?
faq.answer: Yes. It generates AMap links for POI, route, travel plan, and heatmap views.

faq.question: Does it run without network access?
faq.answer: Only basic link generation works offline. API search and routing require access to AMap services.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive location data?
faq.answer: Use caution. API calls and generated links can send location data to AMap services.
